title: Cleisostoma crucicallum
taxonomy: Cleisostoma crucicallum belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a species of orchid. This genus is part of the subtribe Aeridinae, which contains many species of orchids. Cleisostoma crucicallum is native to Asia, and its classification is based on its unique characteristics and morphology. Within the family Orchidaceae, Cleisostoma crucicallum is closely related to other species of orchids in the same subtribe. The classification of Cleisostoma crucicallum is subject to ongoing research and taxonomic revisions.

intro: Cleisostoma crucicallum, also known as the Cross-Bearing Cleisostoma, is a species of orchid native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma crucicallum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma crucicallum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as being incorporated into various folk remedies and traditional formulations.
